methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $200M - $3.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

data.narrative:
LanzaTech operates in the Climate & Energy sector and is currently public (ticker: LNZA) with a stated valuation of $800M+. The peer universe consists of 12 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, comprising 1 public and 8 private comparables. Notable private peers include QuantumScape, Redwood Materials, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems, while public comparables include Form Energy ($3.5B), Sila Nanotechnologies ($3.3B), and Ascend Elements ($3.0B).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, yields an implied valuation of $850M based on the 12-company cohort. The peer set ranges from $200M at the low end to $3.5B at the high end, reflecting the broad valuation distribution within early-stage climate tech. This $850M median aligns closely with LanzaTech's stated $800M+ valuation and incorporates comparable companies across multiple development stages (Series C through Series G).

Synthesizing available methods supports a valuation range of $722M to $977M, anchored at $850M. The narrow range reflects reliance on a single triangulation method. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity of peer business models and commercialization timelines within climate tech, as well as limited public market data (single comparable). Additional revenue multiples or discounted cash flow analysis would strengthen conviction in any point estimate within this band.
